findings.text: Napier stated arrears of £4,605.55 on 8 Apr 2026 and £5,526.66 on 20 Apr 2026, with no itemization.
The 20 Apr 2026 letter demands payment within 5 days and references a 9 May 2026 Notice to Quit deadline.
The £921.11 increase between 8 Apr and 20 Apr suggests additional charges or a different calculation basis.
No itemized breakdown, payment history, or contract terms are in the provided sources; a formal request is needed.

proposed_artifacts.content:
Dear Ms Donnelly,

I refer to your emails of 8 April 2026 and 20 April 2026 regarding accommodation arrears. I note the amounts stated (£4,605.55 and £5,526.66) but require an itemized breakdown to understand the debt.

Please provide:
- A statement of account showing all charges, payments, and the calculation of the current balance.
- The period covered by each charge.
- Details of any missed instalments and the dates they were due.
- A copy of the relevant tenancy agreement and payment schedule.

I also note the 5-day deadline and the 9 May 2026 Notice to Quit deadline. Please clarify how these deadlines interact and confirm that no enforcement action will be taken while I seek advice and attempt to resolve this matter.

I look forward to your prompt response.

Yours sincerely,

Anfal Elbushra
proposed_artifacts.title: Draft request for itemized arrears breakdown

proposed_tasks.instructions: Review the itemized breakdown once received. Compare charges against the tenancy agreement and payment records. Identify any discrepancies or unauthorized charges. Flag any missing payments or credits.
proposed_tasks.title: Verify arrears calculation and reconcile amounts

redaction_flags.description: Student email address (40805511@live.napier.ac.uk) and personal email (albshryanfal4@gmail.com) are visible.
Student name and address (Castlelaw House, Slateford Road, Edinburgh) appear in NAP-SRC-0048.
University staff contact details (phone, email) are present in both sources.

risk_flags.description: Unitemized debt may include errors or unauthorized charges; payment without verification risks overpayment.
Multiple deadlines (5-day and 9 May) could cause confusion and missed compliance.
The Notice to Quit enforcement may proceed without a verified debt, leading to eviction.

summary: Two Napier emails state arrears of £4,605.55 (8 Apr 2026) and £5,526.66 (20 Apr 2026) without itemization. No breakdown, payment history, or contract terms are provided. A formal request for an itemized statement is needed to verify the debt and support any dispute or payment plan.

uncertainties.description: The basis for the arrears amounts (e.g., rent periods, charges, payments) is unknown.
It is unclear whether the 5-day deadline and the 9 May deadline are consistent or conflicting.
The student's payment history and any prior disputes are not evidenced in the sources.
